[rocshmem] functional_tests/driver.sh: add feature to specify custom test config (#4700)

## Motivation

It is not currently possible to use the functional test driver script
to run a single test with a custom configuration.
This PR adds this feature, which can be used as follows:
```Shell
# rocshmem_functional_driver.sh <test_executable> "<test_name> <ranks> <workgroups> <threads> [max_msg_size]" <log_dir>
cd rocshmem_build_dir
mkdir functional_test_logs
# putnbi test with 2 PEs, 32 workgroups, 256 threads per workgroup, and up to 512B per message
./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_driver.sh ./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_tests "putnbi 2 32 256 512" functional_test_logs
less functional_test_logs/putnbi_n2_w32_z256_512B.log
# amo_finc test with 2 PEs, 1 workgroup, and 1024 threads per workgroup
./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_driver.sh ./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_tests "amo_finc 2 1 1024" functional_test_logs
less functional_test_logs/amo_finc_n2_w1_z1024.log
```

## Technical Details

Extend the driver script to handle arbitrary single test configurations
in the same format as used for input to the ExecTest script function:
* If `$TEST` doesn't match any preset,
  `$TEST` is parsed into a bash array `TEST_OPTS=($TEST)`;
  this is implicitly split at whitespace boundary by bash
* Index 0 is the test name, `NAME=${TEST_OPTS[0]}`
* If the length of the array is 4 or 5 then indices 1–4 are parsed
  as ranks, workgroups, threads, and max message size
* Otherwise the same static default configuration as before is used:
  2 ranks, 1 workgroup, 1 thread, and a max message size of 8B

## JIRA ID

AIROCSHMEM-356

## Test Plan

Run a variety of test configurations using the new custom test feature.

## Test Result

Feature has been in internal use for a few months with no apparent
issues or regressions.

## Submission Checklist

- [x] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

fix: prevent negative USD values in ScenarioEditor

Four defensive fixes for the %/USD mode toggle:

1. Disable USD/PLN toggle when no reference price — prevents toggling
   before stock data loads, which left percentage values in fixed-mode
   fields (root cause of negative dollar amounts and overflow).

2. Early return in toggle functions when currentPrice/Rate <= 0 —
   belt-and-suspenders guard matching the disabled button state.

3. Clamp toDelta output to >= -100% — a stock/FX can never drop more
   than 100%, so any input producing a delta below -100 is capped.
   This prevents impossible negative end values in calculations.

4. Add min=0 on fixed-mode inputs — browser validation hint that
   absolute prices/rates cannot be negative.

commit.message:
Fix aspire start failing in VS Code integrated terminal (#15980)

* Fix aspire start failing in VS Code integrated terminal

When 'aspire start' runs from the VS Code Aspire terminal, the detached
child process inherits ASPIRE_EXTENSION_* environment variables, causing
it to detect extension mode and delegate back to the extension via
StartDebugSessionAsync instead of launching the AppHost. This results in
an immediate exit with code 0.

Fix:
- Strip extension-related env vars from the detached child process in
  DetachedProcessLauncher (Unix: remove from startInfo.Environment,
  Windows: build filtered environment block for CreateProcessW)
- Add --non-interactive guard in RunCommand to skip extension delegation
  when running as a child of 'aspire start'
- Set isExtensionHost=false in StartCommand so Dashboard URL always
  shows in 'aspire start' output
- Fix column width calculation in RenderAppHostSummary to only include
  labels that are actually displayed
- Add missing KnownConfigNames constants for extension env vars

Fixes #15786

* Fix detached child env filtering

Preserve DEBUG_SESSION_* values when the detached child CLI is launched from the VS Code Aspire terminal so the AppHost keeps IDE execution and dashboard debug integration. Also address follow-up review issues in StartCommand and the Windows filtered environment block builder.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: prefix-based env var filter, remove unused usings

* Restore using Aspire.Cli.Utils needed for ICliUpdateNotifier

* Add context comment explaining why isExtensionHost is always false

commit.message:
fix: mode mismatch when switching models in USD/PLN display

When ScenarioEditor was in fixed-value mode (USD/PLN) and the user
switched prediction models, the useEffect that syncs localValues from
the new scenarios always wrote raw percentage deltas regardless of
display mode.  This caused percentage values (e.g. -15) to appear as
USD prices, and toDelta() to compute deltas like -107.5%, producing
nonsensical results.

The fix converts percentage deltas to the current display mode (absolute
USD/PLN prices) when syncing from an external scenarios change, using
the same conversion logic as toggleStockMode/toggleFxMode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(eks): install VPA and add memory autoscaling for APISIX (#4443)

* feat(eks): install VPA and add memory autoscaling for APISIX

Install the Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA) from the official Kubernetes
autoscaler Helm chart (kubernetes.github.io/autoscaler, chart 0.8.1,
VPA 1.6.0) as a core cluster capability across all EKS clusters.

VPA is configured with all three components enabled:
- recommender: analyses historical usage and generates recommendations
- updater: applies recommendations via in-place resize or eviction
- admissionController: mutates new pod requests at creation time
  (registerWebhook: true, failurePolicy: Ignore for fail-open behaviour)

The updater runs with --feature-gates=InPlaceOrRecreate=true to take
advantage of in-place pod resource updates on K8s 1.33+ (cluster is
currently 1.35), avoiding eviction when memory can be resized in place.

A VerticalPodAutoscaler object is added for the apache-apisix Deployment
targeting memory only (controlledResources: [memory]). CPU scaling
authority is left entirely to the existing HPA, which scales replicas
3→5 at 50% CPU utilisation. Splitting authority avoids the known HPA/VPA
conflict where VPA adjusting CPU requests distorts the HPA's utilisation
denominator.

A PodDisruptionBudget (maxUnavailable: 1) is enabled on the APISIX Helm
release to ensure at most one gateway pod is disrupted at a time if the
updater falls back to eviction.

The QA stack memory floor is raised to 1500Mi so the initial static
request (and VPA minAllowed) reflects observed actual usage. VPA will
continue to right-size from there based on real utilisation data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/ol_infrastructure/infrastructure/aws/eks/apisix_official.py

Co-authored-by: gemini-code-assist[bot] <176961590+gemini-code-assist[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(eks): use minAvailable=2 for APISIX PDB instead of maxUnavailable=1

Switching from maxUnavailable to minAvailable biases toward maintaining
a minimum serving pool at any replica count. With maxUnavailable=1 the
budget becomes unnecessarily strict as the HPA scales pods above 3;
minAvailable=2 keeps at least 2 pods available while allowing wider
disruption tolerance at higher replica counts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: pin setup-ocaml and cache catala in opam switch

- Pin ocaml/setup-ocaml@v3 to full commit SHA (fefd9c2) — consistent
  with other pinned actions in the file
- Move catala.1.1.0 into opam-packages so it is installed inside the
  switch and included in the opam cache key; subsequent CI runs with a
  cache hit skip all opam network downloads entirely

Root cause of recent failure: transient curl exit-56 downloading
cmdliner.2.1.0 from erratique.ch. Once the switch is cached the job
never contacts erratique.ch again on a cache hit.

commit.message:
headlamp: bypass forward-auth for OIDC endpoints

The authentik forward-auth middleware was intercepting /oidc and /oidc-callback
requests, starting its own auth flow before Headlamp's OIDC handler could run.
This created a 5-6 redirect chain that prevented the OIDC token exchange from
completing.

Split IngressRoute into two rules:
- /oidc and /oidc-callback: skip forward-auth (Headlamp handles OIDC natively)
- All other paths: keep forward-auth for access control

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve all CI test failures (desktop compile + SoundCloud + GloTorrents)

- SearchMediator.java: pass tsr.getHttpHeaders() as 10th arg to
  TellurideSearchResult constructor — was missing after httpHeaders param
  was added in the YouTube headers pipeline, breaking desktop compilation

- SoundCloudConfigFetcher: validate fetched credentials against the
  SoundCloud API before caching; fall back to built-in defaults when
  the remote update server returns stale/expired credentials.
  Updated DEFAULT_CLIENT_ID to a fresh working client_id.

- SoundcloudSearchPattern / SoundcloudItem: drop the 'downloadable'
  flag requirement from isValidSearchResult(). The SoundCloud API now
  returns downloadable=false for virtually all tracks even when they
  have progressive stream URLs; requiring it filtered out 100% of results.

- GloTorrentsSearchPatternTest: @Disabled — gtso.cc is a parked domain
  served by ParkLogic, making this live integration test always fail.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
